Elon opened to 300 from the button and I’m in the big blind with Kc9c (59BB effective vs his ~195BB). K9s is a playable hand heads-up but out of position; it’s too strong to fold and not strong enough to 3-bet profitably out of position against a wide but competent opener. A min 3-bet to 500 would have little fold equity and a larger 3-bet commits too much of my stack. I’ll call to realize equity, keep the pot controllable, and play postflop (add 200 more chips; 300 total committed).

Flop Kd As Qs with Kc9c — I have top pair but a mediocre kicker and there are a lot of hands in the button’s opening range that beat me (any A♠/Ax) and some that I beat (Kx, Qx, broadways). I called preflop to keep the pot controllable; now I want to extract value from worse Ks/Qs/broadways and deny equity to two-card spade combos and other floating hands. A half-pot sizing gives fold equity and charges draws while keeping worse hands in. I’ll bet ~1/2 pot (300 into 650). Plan: if he folds I take it; if he calls I re-evaluate on the turn; if he raises large (polarizing sizing) I will usually give up K9 and avoid bloating the pot with a vulnerable kicker.

Preflop raise with JJ was standard from button. Flop Kd As Qs is a disastrous board for JJ: it's uncoordinated with high cards that crush my hand, two spades for flush draw. Minni's BB call preflop gives her a wide range including Ax, Kx, Qx, broadway, suited connectors. Her donk-bet of 300 into ~650 pot is small but polarizes her range to value (top pair+, sets, straights) or bluffs/draws, but JJ blocks few bluffs and has only ~2 outs to two pair (remaining Js). Equity is poor (~15-20% vs her range), pot odds 950:300 (~3.2:1) require ~24% equity to call profitably, which I don't have. No position advantage postflop, villain aggressive historically but this spot face-up. Folding preserves stack for better spots; JJ plays well in position uncontested.

KTo is a strong starting hand on the Button in a 3-handed game. I will raise to 300 to exert pressure on the blinds and take the initiative, which is consistent with my established raising range and sizing in previous hands.

I have a medium-strong pocket pair (9♣9♦) in the small blind facing a button open to 300 with the big blind yet to act. With ~63.5bb effective, I prefer to 3-bet for value and isolation rather than limp/call and play a multiway pot out of position. Flash on the button opens frequently and will have a fairly wide range, so a 3-bet will get folds from lots of his weaker holdings and charge speculative hands. I’ll raise to a size (~1000 total) that gives decent fold equity and discourages loose calls from the big blind, while leaving room to maneuver postflop. If the big blind 3-bets large I’ll re-evaluate (often folding to an oversized 3-bet, or calling smaller 3-bets depending on price), but for now take the initiative and 3-bet to isolate and extract value.

Q8o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability. Facing a 3-bet from Minni after Flash's button open, the pot is 1400 and I need to call 900 (9bb effectively). My hand has no showdown value against typical 3-bet ranges, low equity multiway if Flash calls, and Minni has only 5400 behind making fold equity irrelevant for calling. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

I opened to 300 with KTo from the Button, and Minni 3-bet to 1000 from the Small Blind. In a 3-handed game, KTo is a decent hand, but Minni has shown significant aggression in previous hands (e.g., Hand #4 where she 3-bet large and took it down, and Hand #21 where she played JJ very aggressively). My stack is comfortable at 13,750, but calling a 3-bet out of position (or even in position, which I am here) against a relatively short-stacked player who is playing tight-aggressive ranges can be tricky. However, folding KTo to a single 3-bet in a 3-handed game might be too weak. I will call to see a flop, as I have position and the hand has enough high-card value to continue, especially since the pot odds are 2:1.

I have 9♣9♦ — a clean overpair to the 7♥6♥7♣ flop. Flash called my 3‑bet preflop so his range is wide (lots of Kx/Ax, suited connectors and heart combos, some small pairs and the occasional 7). This board is moderately wet (paired sevens + two hearts), so I want to both extract value from worse one‑pair hands and charge heart/straight draws. Betting is the right play: a c‑bet around half to a little over half the pot charges draws while keeping worse hands and many bluffs in. I will bet 1,200 into the 2,100 pot (~57%) — big enough to deny free cards and get value, but small enough to allow me to call a reasonable raise and re-evaluate on the turn. Plan: if he calls I’ll continue to evaluate turn texture (barrel safe cards, check/control on dangerous hearts/7s); if he check‑raises large (polarized) I will give significant weight to folding since trips/sets are the most likely strong hands here.

Minni 3-bet pre-flop and is now firing a strong continuation bet of 1200 into a 2100 pot on a paired board [7h 6h 7c]. My King-high has very little equity against her likely range of overpairs (88-AA) or high-card hands like AK/AQ that still beat me. Given her stack size relative to the pot, she is likely to shove the turn if I call. Without a pair or a strong draw, I cannot justify calling such a large bet in this spot. I will fold and preserve my stack for better opportunities.

3-handed on the button I should be opening a very wide range. 9♦5♠ is marginal but fine as a button open — the goal is to take the blinds with position and initiative. Min raise is 200 but a standard open to 300 gives more fold equity and avoids enabling limp/call play; the cost is small relative to my 7,500 stack. Plan: raise to take the lead; fold to a large 3‑bet (I have poor hand equity vs a 3‑betting range). If called, use position to c‑bet favorable flops and control pot size on dry boards.

J4o is a trash hand with no high card strength, poor connectivity, and offsuit. Facing a button raise preflop from the small blind, I'm out of position postflop, making it difficult to play profitably even against a wide range. Standard fold to avoid spew and preserve stack for better spots.

I am in the Big Blind with King-Eight offsuit. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button, and the Small Blind has folded. The pot odds are 3.25-to-1 (200 to call for a total pot of 650), which requires roughly 23.5% equity. King-Eight offsuit typically has over 50% equity against a wide Button opening range. While playing out of position against an aggressive player like Minni is challenging, the hand is strong enough to defend given the price. I will call to see the flop.

I have flopped top pair with a King on a very dry board (K-3-2 rainbow). I am out of position against the pre-flop aggressor, Minni. In this situation, the standard play is to check to the raiser to allow them to continuation bet with their entire range, including bluffs. Since the board is so disconnected, I am not worried about many draws, and checking allows me to control the pot size with a medium-strength top pair.

I opened a wide range from the button and this flop (2c Kh 3s) is very dry — it misses most of the BB’s calling range. I have no pair or meaningful draw with 9♦5♠, but I have initiative and good fold equity. A standard continuation bet of about half pot should take this down often while keeping the pot manageable if called. I’ll size to ~1/2 pot (325). If I face strong resistance (a sizable raise) I’ll fold this weak holding; if called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and only continue with a pair, a clean draw, or a favorable runout.

I have flopped top pair with a King on an extremely dry board (K-3-2 rainbow). Minni's 1/3 pot continuation bet is standard for her range, which includes many bluffs, underpairs, and some better or worse Kings. Raising here would likely force out her bluffs and only get called by hands that have me beaten (like stronger Kings or sets). By calling, I keep her range wide, control the pot size with my medium-strength top pair, and allow her to continue betting on future streets with air or weaker hands. I will check-call this flop.

The turn brings a second King, giving me three of a kind. Since I am out of position against Minni, the pre-flop aggressor who also bet the flop, checking is the most effective play. It serves two purposes: it allows her to continue bluffing with hands that missed the board and it keeps the pot controlled in case she has a stronger King (like AK, KQ, or KJ) or a rare full house (33 or 22). Given that there is only one King remaining in the deck, it is highly likely I have the best hand, and checking is the best way to extract value from her wider range.

I have 9♦5♠ and was the preflop raiser; I c-bet the flop and was called. The turn pairs the King (2♣ K♥ 3♠ → K♦) and the caller now checks. I have no pair and no meaningful draw — betting the turn would be a pure bluff. Villain called my flop c-bet, which indicates a fairly sticky range (Kx, pocket pairs, or other marginal hands) that often calls turn bets as well. The pot is only 1,300 and a bluff-sized bet here gives the caller good odds to continue, so I have limited fold equity and a high chance of being called by better hands. I’ll take the low-variance line and check back to control the pot and see a river. On the river I’ll re-evaluate: fire a bluff only if the card and villain’s line make it credible; fold to significant aggression if I miss.

On this board of 2c-Kh-3s-Kd-Ac, I have trips (Kings). The river Ace is an interesting card. It's a card that hits both of our ranges, but Minni checked back the turn, which often indicates she might be giving up on a bluff or has a hand with some showdown value that didn't want to face a check-raise. If she has an Ace, she might call a small value bet, or she might check it back herself if I check. However, checking allows her to potentially bluff if she missed, or check behind with a weak pair. Given the pot size is 1300, a small bet might look like a 'blocking bet' or a weak lead, enticing a call from an Ace or even a bluff-raise. However, check-calling is also a very safe way to capture bluffs. Since the board is so dry and I have a strong hand that's likely the best, I will check to see if she wants to fire a final barrel or show down a hand like a small pocket pair or an Ace.

Board: 2♣ K♥ 3♠ K♦ A♣. I hold 9♦5♠ — no pair or meaningful blocker. I c‑bet the dry flop and was called; both checked the turn. Flash’s line (call on flop, check turn) represents a sticky middle/strong range (Kx, pocket pairs, some Ax) that will rarely fold to a river bluff, especially after an Ace. The pot is only 1,300 and my fold equity is low, so a bluff is thin and likely to be called or raised. I take the low‑variance line to control the pot and check back; I’ll fold to significant river aggression since I have nothing.

Preflop on the button with QhTs offsuit in a limped pot (pot 150, to call BB 100). QTo is a strong hand from the button due to position, decent equity against calling ranges, and fold equity against the blinds. Opponents: Flash (SB, 136BB) calls sometimes but folds to aggression; Minni (BB, 67BB effective) is aggressive but short-stacked. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) applies pressure, builds pot in position, and has good steal potential. Folding is too tight; calling is suboptimal OOP postflop. History shows blinds often fold to button raises.

I am in the Small Blind with Jack-Five offsuit. Elon, the chip leader, raised to 300 from the Button. J5o is a very weak hand to play out of position against a Button open. Calling would put me in a difficult spot post-flop, and 3-betting as a bluff is not ideal with this hand strength and stack depth. The most disciplined play is to fold and wait for a better opportunity.

I’m in the big blind (100 posted) facing a single-button open to 300. I hold 8♠6♥ — a marginal, non‑suited connector but playable versus a wide button opening range. Calling 200 more to make it 300 gives me ~3.25:1 pot odds (need ≈23–24% equity), which is reasonable versus a standard button range and lets me realize equity and play post‑flop without overcommitting. A min 3‑bet to 500 is a thin bluff with poor postflop playability and unnecessarily bloats the pot against a deeper opponent; folding is too tight versus a single raise. I’ll defend by calling and evaluate the flop for continuation, bluffing, or folding based on texture and opponent actions.

I called preflop to realize equity; flop comes Jh Js 6c and I hold 8♠6♥ — bottom pair with a weak kicker. Pot is 650 and stacks are deep enough that I don’t want to inflate the pot with a marginal hand that is behind against a BTN opening range (QQ+, 6x, A6/K6 combos, and the occasional 66). Betting here would often get called by better hands and fold out only pure air; checking keeps the pot under control, lets villain bluff with missed broadways, and allows me to call a reasonable c‑bet to get to a cheap showdown. Plan: check and call small-to-medium continuation bets; fold to large raises or heavy pressure. Check for now.

Preflop raise with QTo from button was standard for position and fold equity. Flop Jh Js 6c is a paired board that hits my button raising range well (Jx, Ax, sets, overpairs to J). Minni's BB calling range vs button raise includes suited connectors, pockets, broadways, but checking first in suggests weakness or trap. No showdown value with queen-high, no draws, so c-bet for value against weaker pairs/draws and fold equity against air/floats. History shows Minni calls multi-street but folds to aggression postflop (e.g., Hand 16, 20, 29). Half-pot bet sizes well, pot 650, stacks effective ~65BB (Minni), builds pot OOP while protecting. Bet 400 total (no prior bet).

I’m on the BB with 8♠6♥ and checked the flop; Elon c-bet 400 into a 650 pot (pot now 1050). I have second pair (6s) with a weak kicker — a hand with showdown value vs a wide BTN c-bet range but vulnerable to Jx (which makes trips), overpairs, and large turn pressure. Calling 400 puts 400 into a 1050 pot (400/(1050+400) ≈ 27.6% required equity), which is reasonable: my hand has enough showdown equity against a typical continuation range and calling keeps the pot controlled. A raise would be a thin bluff that bloats the pot and gets called by better hands; folding is too passive because I beat a lot of pure bluffs and weaker broadways. Plan: call now, re-evaluate on the turn — fold to very large barrels unless I improve or pick up a strong read, value/raise if I improve to two‑pair/trips.

I hold second pair (6♥) with a poor kicker on Jh Js 6c 9c. The pot is 1,450 and this is a marginal hand that wants to control the pot size rather than build it in position against an aggressive BTN. Checking keeps opponent’s weaker hands and bluffs in the pot, preserves my showdown equity, and avoids committing chips when I’m likely behind to Jx, 9x, overpairs or a club flush. Plan: check here; call small-to-medium turn bets (rough guideline ~up to ½–⅔ pot depending on sizing/read) and fold to large, polarized barrels. If Elon checks back I will re-evaluate the river and look for a cheap showdown or a small value/bluff depending on the runout.

Turn board brings the 9c, completing the club flush draw and adding potential straight draws like 7-8-10. My QhTs is still queen-high with no pair, no club draw, and only a weak backdoor spade flush draw. Minni's check-check after calling flop c-bet suggests a medium strength hand like a weaker jack (KJ, TJ), pocket pair below jacks, or a missed draw now checking for pot control. I have no value to bet against her calling range, and betting risks getting raised by better or drawing dead. Fold equity is low after her flop call on paired board. Checking behind takes a free card (river can't complete nut flush since no clubs held), maintains pot control with showdown value against total air (unlikely), and keeps her guessing for potential river bluff if checked to me. Position advantage allows seeing river cheap. Consistent with history where Minni shows down medium strength after multi-checks (e.g., Hand 25, 30). No reason to bloat pot OOP for her with nothing.

Board: Jh Js 6c 9c Qs. I hold 8s6h — second pair (sixes) with a poor kicker. Elon bet the flop and then checked the turn; he checked the river as well, giving me a free look. There is minimal value to extract — Jx, Qx and overpairs will call a river bet and pure air will fold, so a bluff has very little fold equity and a thin chance of success. Checking preserves my stack and realizes showdown equity against missed broadways and weaker holdings; this is the lowest-variance, most +EV line given the action and board. I’ll check to showdown.

River Qs pairs no board cards but completes potential broadway straight draws (e.g., 78T for 789TJ straight) and adds a spade flush draw, though primary club draw from flop/turn missed (board has two clubs). My QhTs is now top pair weak kicker (queen-high effectively with T kicker), but no pair—wait, Qh is unpaired queen-high no kicker relevant. Board: paired Js Jh, so trips J dominate. Minni's check-call flop on dry paired board (Jh Js 6c) requires Jx (AJ,KJ,TJ,QJ, pockets unlikely), sets (66,JJ), or strong draws (78 suited clubs). Her turn check after 9c (straight/flush card) and river check suggest medium strength like weaker Jx (T9s missed straight, but called flop), pocket below J, or missed draw without value bet. My queen-high has thin showdown value vs pure air/missed draws (e.g., flush misses, weak 87), but crushed by Jx trips (80%+ range). Position allows free showdown; betting risks: low fold equity (she called flop, pot-committed ~4BB effective stack 6175/1450~4x pot), value called by better Qx/Jx, raised off equity. History: Minni passive post-check (Hands 25,30,43 check-check showdowns medium/weak); checks down worse often (Hand 20). Checking maximizes EV: pot control, realizes equity vs worse, avoids spew OOP. No bluff value (weak blocker to QJ, thin air rep post-check turn). Check behind.

I am on the button with 5s3c, which is a very weak hand. Even though it is only 100 more to call, this hand plays poorly post-flop and doesn't offer much in terms of equity or playability against two active opponents. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I’m in the small blind with Kc9h heads-up vs the big blind (button folded). Pot is 150 and I only need to put 50 more to call, but K9o is a good heads-up opening hand and I don’t want to see flops out of position for cheap. Standard, table-consistent sizing is ~3x the BB — it gives fold equity and reduces the times I’m forced to play out of position postflop. My stack (6125) is deep enough to apply pressure and maneuver postflop, but K9o is not a hand I want to get 3-bet all-in with; I’ll fold to large 3-bets and evaluate small/medium 3-bets by sizing and pot odds. Plan: raise to 300 total to take the initiative, c-bet favorable flops and proceed cautiously on coordinated boards or heavy resistance.

K3o is a marginal hand at best from the big blind facing a small blind raise. The hand has poor playability out of position, low kicker with king-high, and limited potential. Pot odds are decent (3:1), but post-flop will be difficult OOP against Minni's likely wide range. Minni has shown aggression in prior hands with speculative holdings, but defending trash like this leaks value long-term. Deep stacks favor selective defense. Standard fold to preserve stack for premium situations.

A♠K♣ on the button is a top-tier opening hand. With 62 BB effective stacks and only the blinds to face, I want to take the initiative, deny correct odds to limpers/callers and build a pot to play postflop in position. A standard 3x BB open to 300 balances value and fold equity — big enough to fold out many speculative hands and isolate one opponent, but not so big as to commit my stack preflop. If I face a 3-bet I'll decide between a 4-bet or a call based on sizing and opponent tendencies; for now open to 300.

Preflop in small blind facing a button raise to 300 (250 to call into 450 pot). My hand 5d3h is uncoordinated trash with no high card value, straight potential, or suitedness. Poor playability postflop OOP against likely wide button range from Minni. Standard fold to preserve stack and avoid difficult spots.

I am in the Big Blind with King-Deuce suited. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. While K2s is a marginal hand, the pot odds are 3.25:1 (calling 200 to win a pot of 650). In a 3-handed game, K2s is strong enough to defend the Big Blind against a button raise, especially with the suited potential and the high card value of the King. I will call and see a flop.

I have bottom pair with a King kicker (Deuce of Diamonds) on a 10d-6c-2s rainbow board. Minni, who raised pre-flop from the button, has checked behind or I am first to act. Checking is the most standard play here to see how the opponent reacts and to keep the pot small with a marginal hand. I have some showdown value but am vulnerable to many overcards.

I opened A♠K♣ to 300 preflop to take the initiative. The flop (6♣ T♦ 2♠) is very dry and misses my hand, but I'm in position against a single BB caller. A continuation bet is the standard line here: it folds out many unpaired hands, small pockets and speculative holdings, preserves the initiative, and leverages my two overcards/backdoor outs. I size to ~60% of the pot (400 into 650) — big enough to deny correct odds to lots of draws/weak calls but small enough not to overcommit if I face heavy resistance. If called I'll reevaluate on the turn and give up to a large raise unless I improve.

I have bottom pair with a King kicker on a relatively dry board. Minni, the pre-flop aggressor on the button, has made a continuation bet of 400 into a 650 pot. This is a standard sized bet. In a three-handed game, bottom pair is often enough to continue against a single bet, especially when I have overcards that could improve to top pair (though the King of diamonds doesn't help with a flush on this board, it's still a high card). Folding here would be too exploitable. Raising is unnecessary and turns my hand into a bluff, whereas calling allows me to keep her bluffs in and see how the turn develops. I will call the 400.

On the turn, the 7 of diamonds has significantly improved my equity by giving me a King-high flush draw to go along with my bottom pair. I checked the flop and called a continuation bet, and I will continue to check here. Checking allows me to see how Minni proceeds; if she bets, I have a very easy call with my pair and strong draw, and if she checks back, I get a free card to potentially hit my flush or improve to two pair/trips.

Check. I opened A♠K♣ and c‑bet the dry flop; Villain called. The 7♦ on the turn doesn’t improve my hand and actually helps parts of Villain’s calling range (7x, 8‑9 straights, and it brings a second diamond), while I only have 6 clean outs (three Aces + three Kings) to hit the river (~12–13%). Betting the turn risks getting called or check‑raised by hands that already beat me (T‑x, pocket pairs, sets, made straights) and would not fold often enough to make a bluff profitable. I want to control the pot with marginal equity, see a free river, and then decide: call a small river bet for the correct odds or fold to significant aggression.

On the river, the board is 6c-Td-2s-7d-Qs. I have a pair of deuces with a King kicker. I missed my flush draw that picked up on the turn. After I checked and called a flop bet, Minni checked back on the turn, which suggests she either has a marginal hand she wants to take to showdown (like a 6, 7, or middle pocket pair) or she is giving up on a bluff. The Queen on the river is an overcard that could have hit her range, but it's more likely she still holds a marginal hand. I have very little value in betting here, as better hands are unlikely to fold and worse hands won't call. I will check and hope to win at showdown against her air or missed draws.

Preflop I opened A♠K♣ to 300 and c‑bet the very dry flop (6♣ T♦ 2♠) for 400; Villain called. Both of us checked the 7♦ turn, and Villain now checks the Q♠ river. I have no pair — only Ace‑high — so I have almost no showdown value against the parts of Villain’s range that called the flop (T‑x, pocket pairs, 7x, 6x, some Ax). Betting here would be a pure bluff trying to fold out medium pairs and missed hands, but Villain’s passive line (call flop, check turn) suggests many of those hands will either call a rational river bluff or already beat me. Fold equity is thin and a successful bluff would have to fold hands that are unlikely to have shown this line. Pot is 1,450 and I don’t want to risk a large chunk of my 5,575 stack on a low‑EV bluff. Checking controls the pot, lets me see showdown for free, and preserves stack equity for later spots. I check.

Preflop on the button with K4o after SB folds and BB checks. Pot has 150 (dead SB + BB), excellent position heads-up vs short-stacked BB (54BB). K4o is a strong stealing hand here: king blocker, decent high card equity if called. Standard button raise wide to steal dead money and BB. Minni raises often but folds to aggression frequently from history. Raise to 300 (3x BB) for good fold equity and pot-building if called, maintaining position postflop.

I am in the Small Blind with 6s2c, a very weak hand. Elon, on the button, has raised to 300. With 62o, I am out of position and have very little equity against a standard button opening range. Given the weak nature of my hand and the fact that I would be playing a bloated pot out of position, the standard and most profitable play is to fold.

Elon opened to 300 from the button; I’m in the big blind with Q♦5♣. This is a marginal hand (dominated by a lot of button’s calling/raising range and poor kicker playability), but defending is cheap — I only need to commit 200 more (300 total) into a ~450-pot, giving ~30% break-even odds to see a flop. Elon opens frequently from the button, so his range is likely wide enough that calling is profitable enough here. 3-betting as a bluff with Q5o is thin (I’m OOP postflop and could face a 4-bet or a call and then be in a tough spot), and folding outright surrenders good pot odds. Plan: call to see the flop, proceed cautiously postflop — continue only with clear improvements or strong equity/draws; fold to heavy action with just a weak pair or dominated top-pair situations.

I have top pair (Q♦5♣) on a very draw-heavy flop (Q♥ J♥ 7♥). This is a good but vulnerable hand — two-heart holdings by Elon would already be a made flush and single-heart hands still have strong backdoor equity. Elon opened wide from the button, so his range contains many worse Qx/Jx and broadways that I beat. I want to deny free cards and charge heart/straight draws while extracting value from worse pairs. A ~1/2 pot c-bet (~350 into 650, making the pot ≈1000) accomplishes that: it forces many marginal hands/draws to pay ~35% to continue. Plan: bet 350. If he folds I take the pot; if he calls I re-evaluate on the turn (proceed cautiously if a heart or a straight-completing card arrives). I will fold to a large check-raise or sizable raise (likely a made flush or two-pair) and consider calling small, non-committal raises if they look like bluffs or thin calls.

Nut flush draw with K♥ blocker on a monotone heart flop (J♥ Q♥ 7♥). This hand has ~35% equity against villain's calling preflop and leading flop range, which likely includes weaker heart draws (e.g., low hearts), gutshots, floats, or overpairs without hearts. Position on button heads-up, excellent pot odds for a call (350 into 1000+), but raising maximizes EV: extracts value from worse draws, protects against straight completions (e.g., T or 8-9), applies fold equity given villain's history of folding to aggression (e.g., Hands 4, 5, 10+), and leverages my 4:1 stack advantage (19.7k vs 4.9k). Sizing to 1200 (~1.2x pot total bet) balances value and fold equity without overcommitting. Folding impossible, check unavailable.

I bet 350 on the flop and Elon then raised to 1200, so I must call 850 into a 2200 pot. Calling 850 would require ~27.9% equity (850 / (2200+850)) and would commit roughly 1200 total on this hand. I have Q♦5♣ — top pair with a very weak kicker and no hearts on a three-hearts board (Qh Jh 7h). Elon's large raise polarizes his range toward strong value hands (two-heart combos = made flushes, sets like JJ/77, QJ/two-pair, or very strong Qx), many of which dominate me; there are few realistic bluffs here. I'm out of position and would be risking a large portion of my stack with a hand that has limited improvement equity against a flush-heavy/value-heavy range. Folding preserves my stack for better spots, so I fold.

I am on the button with K2o. While it is a marginal hand, in a three-handed game, raising from the button to steal the blinds is a standard play. However, given the recent aggression from Minni (who has been 3-betting and leading out frequently) and Elon (who has been calling or raising frequently), K2o is likely too weak to play for a raise here if I expect to be 3-bet or called out of position. Comparing my stack of 14,375 to the current pot, there is no need to force a marginal steal. Folding is the safest and most disciplined play to maintain my stack for a better spot.

Pot is 150 and I’ve already posted the 50 SB; calling another 50 (total 100 committed) gives me 3:1 pot odds to see the flop. Tc5s is a weak, marginal hand and I’ll be out of position vs Elon post‑flop, so I don’t want to build a bigger pot by raising with such a hand. A raise is better reserved for stronger holdings or a planned bluff vs multiple players — Elon has a large stack and will defend widely or 3‑bet, which I don’t want to face with T5o. I’ll call cheaply to see a flop; plan is to fold to significant aggression unless I improve (top pair, two pair, a strong draw), and otherwise keep the pot small.

Preflop in BB vs SB limp (Flash folded). Hand 3c4s is a suited connector with straight potential and some flush potential. Pot is 200 with dead money, to call 0. Checking is standard to see flop cheaply OOP in a limped pot against a wide SB limping range. Raising risks playing OOP with a marginal hand postflop, and folding throws away equity. Plan to play postflop based on board texture.

I limped preflop with Tc5s and completely missed this flop (Kd 9d 8h). I have no pair and no diamond draw — only 3 tens left for a weak pair that will often be second best. Out of position versus Elon (deep and generally willing to defend/raise), barreling as a bluff OOP is low EV — he will call or raise wide and I don’t want to face that with junk. Pot control is the priority: check to see a turn for free and fold to meaningful aggression. If Elon makes a very small, noncommittal probe I might call occasionally, but otherwise will fold to larger bets.

On the flop of 8h-9d-Kd, my 3c4s has completely missed: no pair, no strong draw (minor gutshot to 5 for a wheel straight, but unlikely and weak), no flush draw realized. The board is king-high with possible straight draws (T-J) and flush draws (diamonds). Facing a limped pot from SB (wide range including low cards, suited connectors, Ax suited), Minni's check suggests weakness—no king or strong draw likely, but she could have showdown value or traps. Betting OOP as bluff is poor: thin value unlikely, fold equity decent but risks calling station or raise, and I'm OOP multi-street. Checking keeps pot small, realizes equity for free on turn (backdoor straight/flush poss.), and allows check-fold/raise if she bets. Consistent with preflop plan to play postflop pot control with speculative hand.

Check. I limped with Tc5s and missed the flop; the turn (6d) pairs the middle and completes a three‑diamond board (Kd,9d,6d). I have no diamonds and only six clean outs (3 tens + 3 fives) — ~24% to improve on the river. Facing one deep opponent who checked flop and turn, bluffing OOP with no equity is low EV and I should keep the pot small. Plan: check to see the river for free; fold to any meaningful bet. Given the current pot (200), I’ll only consider calling very small probe bets (roughly ≤~90 chips) because larger bets give inferior pot odds to my outs. If checked to the river I’ll reevaluate with the actual river card (value bet only if I pair or pick up reasonable equity).

Turn board 6d adds a diamond flush draw and completes some straight draws (e.g., 5-7, 7-10), but my 3c4s remains a complete air hand with no pair, no flush draw, and no straight draw (gutshot to 2-5 or 5-7 unlikely and weak). Minni's SB limp and double check suggests weakness: unlikely to have Kd/strong 9/8, more likely low cards, missed draws, or medium showdown value. Betting OOP as a bluff is -EV: thin/no value, fold equity limited against a limper who might call wide or station, stacks her short stack for a river shove/call spot I'm behind in. Checking realizes zero equity safely, keeps pot tiny (200 into 20k stack), induces bluffs on river if I improve (unlikely), and allows check-fold/raise post-river. Consistent with pot control strategy from flop on a speculative hand that missed entirely.

I hold Tc5s on 8h 9d Kd 6d 4c — I have no pair, no flush and no completed straight. Pot is only 200 and it’s checked to me on the river. With zero showdown value and no realistic way to fold out hands that beat me (Elon checked flop/turn but a river bet will usually be value from Kx/9x/8x or a made straight), bluffing OOP is negative EV against a deep, sticky opponent. I’ll preserve my stack and check; I’ll fold to any river bet.

River 4c pairs my 4s for bottom pair with a 3 kicker. This is thin value in a checked-down limped pot against SB Minni's wide limping range (suited connectors, Ax, broadways, low pairs) that often misses the scary Kd9d8h6d board with flush and 5-7 straight completed. Her quadruple check indicates weakness: unlikely Kx/9x/8x/6x (would bet earlier streets for value/protection), more likely missed draws, A-high, Q-high, or busted connectors that showdown. History shows Minni check-downs weak/medium hands (e.g., Hands 2,25,43,46). Small bet extracts value from unpaired hands that call (station-y tendencies), while min-size 100 risks little relative to deep stacks (mine 20k, hers 4.8k) and pot (200). If raised/shoved, easy fold as behind flushes/straights/TP. Better than check: gains dead money + calls from worse without fold equity reliance. Plan: bet pot control size, fold to raise.

I limped preflop with Tc5s and missed everything on the flop and turn (8h 9d Kd 6d). On the river (4c) I have no pair, no flush, no straight and effectively zero showdown equity versus a reasonable value range. Elon checked flop and turn then bets 100 into the pot — calling 100 here would require roughly 25% equity (100 to win ~400) to break even. There are no cards left to improve and the opponent’s line is consistent with many value hands (Kx/9x/8x or better); while he may bluff sometimes with missed diamond draws, the bluff frequency is too low to justify a call. Raising as a bluff is also negative EV with no blockers and out of position. I’ll preserve my stack and fold to this river bet.

I have Ad8d on the button with pot 150 and only 100 to call. A8s is a standard, playable hand in position: it has the nut-ace blocker, suitedness for flush/straight equity, and great post‑flop playability. With ~48bb effective I prefer to keep the pot manageable and use position rather than bloat the pot preflop and risk a 3‑bet from deeper stacks. Calling gives good pot odds (100 to win ~250 → ~40% equity needed), keeps weaker hands and suited connectors in, and lets me evaluate the flop to either barreI n or fold. Plan: call 100, play aggressively on favorable flops (top pair, strong draw) and proceed cautiously if I miss the board or face heavy action.

Preflop in SB facing a Button limp into a 250 pot, needing 50 more to call. K4o is a speculative hand with a King blocker, decent fold equity against a wide limping range from Minni on the Button. Raising isolates the limper, steals dead money from blinds/limp, and takes initiative OOP. Standard iso-raise size around 3x BB given the limp. Consistent with previous action in similar spot (Hand 47 with K4o raised to 300). Calling risks multiway OOP with poor postflop playability; folding too tight for SB.

I am in the Big Blind holding Qs9s. Minni limped from the button, and Elon raised to 300 from the Small Blind. I am getting 2.5:1 on a call (200 to win a total pot of 500). While Q9o isn't a premium hand, it is strong enough to defend in a three-way pot, especially given my stack depth. Closing the action by calling is standard here as I have reasonable post-flop playability against Elon's range, which likely includes broadways, pairs, and suited connectors.

I’m on the button with Ad8d and have already put in 100. Pot is 700 and it costs me 200 more to call (300 total). A8s has the nut‑ace blocker and diamond equity plus excellent post‑flop position against Elon and Flash. Elon’s open to 300 and a flat from the BB looks like wide ranges; I don’t want to bloat the pot with a small raise that gains little fold equity and can induce 3‑bets from deeper stacks. With ~47bb effective I’ll call 200 more (300 total) to keep the pot manageable, realize equity, and play the flop aggressively on A‑high or diamond/straight draws while exercising pot control or folding to heavy resistance on dry misses.

Preflop raiser OOP on a draw-heavy flop (spade flush draw, wheel straight draws like 36o, gutshots like 67). Bottom pair with strong king kicker has decent showdown value against calling ranges from BB defend and button limp-call, which include floats, weak Jx, suited connectors. C-bet for thin value, protection against draws, and to deny equity. Half-pot size balances value from worse pairs/draws while maintaining fold equity vs better hands. Pot control if checked, but initiative favors betting. Minni short stack (4.5k) more likely to commit if drawing.

I have Q9o on a board of J-5-4 rainbow. I have one overcard to the Jack (the Queen) and a backdoor straight draw. Elon is leading into two players after raising pre-flop, which often indicates a range of pocket pairs, broadway cards (AJ, KJ, QJ), or high-card bluffs. I am currently behind most of his value range and have very little equity to continue against a 1/3 pot bet in a three-way pot. Minni is still behind me to act, which increases the risk of being trapped or facing further aggression. Folding is the most prudent play here as my hand is too weak to call.

Call 450. I’m on the button with Ad8d — ace-high with diamond backdoors but no pair on 4h-5s-Js. Elon opened preflop and made a small-ish continuation bet (~1/3 pot). Calling is the right play: the price is good (≈25% equity needed) to see a turn, I keep worse hands and bluffs in his range, and I have positional leverage to control the pot post‑flop. A raise here would be an ambitious bluff that bloats the pot with almost no immediate equity and hands like Jx/pocket pairs will still continue; folding is too tight given the price and the ace blocker. Plan: call and re-evaluate on the turn — if I pair the A or pick up meaningful equity (diamond/straight draw) I can start betting for value or turn a semi‑bluff; if I miss and face heavy turn aggression I will fold to large pressure to protect my stack.

On the turn, the board is 4h-5s-Js-3c, pairing nothing dangerous but completing some straight draws like 6-2. I hold bottom pair (4s) with King kicker, which has good showdown value against Minni's button limp-call-call range, likely including weak Jx, 5x, 4x, draws (spades, 67), and floats. The 3c adds a low straight completion but doesn't drastically improve her range over mine. With initiative from preflop raise and flop c-bet (Flash folded), continuing with a half-pot bet extracts value from worse hands/draws, protects against equity realization, and denies her cheap river cards. Minni's stack is 4075 into 1800 pot (SPR ~2.3), so she may commit if behind but folds weaker floats. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or allowing her to bluff; betting maintains pressure OOP heads-up. Consistent with flop strategy of thin value/protection. Half-pot size (900) balances range, gets calls from worse, fold equity from air.

I hold Ad8d on the button; board is 4h-5s-Js-3c. Pot is 2700, Elon bets 900. Elon opened preflop after a limp and c-bet the flop, then continues into the turn — his range is weighted toward Jx, some mid pairs/overpairs and some bluffs. I have only ace-high but I block a lot of A‑x combos and have decent showdown value vs his bluffs/missed broadway hands. Calling 900 for a chance to see the river costs ~22% of my stack but gives ~25% pot-odds (900 to win 2700), so it’s the lowest-variance, highest-EV line: keep worse hands and bluffs in, use position to get to showdown or to fold to extreme river aggression. A raise here would be a thin, high-variance bluff against an aggressive, deep opponent and would frequently be called by better Jx/pairs; shoving is too committal without a real draw or pair. Plan: call the 900, re-evaluate on the river — extract value if I pair an Ace, otherwise fold to decisive jams.

River King pairs my Kd, improving me to two pair (Kings and 4s) with the nut 4 kicker effectively. Board: 4h-5s-Js-3c-Kc. Possible draws (spades, wheel 2-6, gutshots) largely miss; King brings top pair for hands like Kx, QJ. Minni's button limp-call pre, call flop c-bet on drawy board, call turn into low SPR shows strength/stuck range: weak Jx, 5x, 4x, missed draws, weaker Kx (KQ,KT,K9). Sets (44 full house, JJ,55,33) possible but narrow in limp-call range. Pot 3600, her stack 3175 (SPR <1.1), betting pot-ish all-in maximizes value from her calling range (worse pairs, top pair, stubborn draws) while denying equity outs or bluffs if checked. Checking risks her checking behind worse or value-betting better; betting extracts max with position disadvantage but initiative from prior aggression. Consistent with thin value/protection strategy now super strong.

Elon just shoved my remaining 3175 into a 6775 pot. Calling 3175 to win a total pot of 9950 requires ~31.9% equity. I hold A♦8♦ — only ace-high with no pair and no completed diamond flush. Elon opened preflop and has barreled flop and turn, then jams the river: that line is heavily weighted to value (Jx, sets, 4s/5s, Kx, two-pair combos) with a relatively small bluff frequency (missed spade/straight draws). My Ace blocker reduces some bluffs but not nearly enough to get to the ~32% break-even equity against a three-street value-heavy range. With ~31.7bb behind this is a classic fold-to-all-in situation — preserve my stack and wait for a better, lower-variance spot. I fold.

On the button with J5s suited connectors, this is a strong speculative hand in position. Pot is 150, to call 100 gives excellent pot odds (1.5:1 direct, better implied). Minni is short-stacked at 3075 total (~30BB effective), likely to defend wide or shove over a raise, creating multiway pots or fold equity. Flash deep but in SB. Standard play is to raise for value/isolation/thin value against limpers or blinds. Raising to 300 (3x BB) builds pot, maintains initiative, and leverages position postflop. Folding or limping too passive in this spot.

I am in the Small Blind with 8d 3c. Elon, on the button, has raised to 300. This hand is very weak and does not play well post-flop, especially out of position against a big stack like Elon. Minni, in the Big Blind, is extremely short-stacked, which might make the hand more volatile. There is no strategic value in calling or raising with such a poor hand. Folding is the standard and correct play here.

I’m in the big blind with pocket 4s (4h4s) facing a button open to 300. Pot is 450 and I need 200 more to call. With ~30bb effective, a min-re-raise to 500 gains little fold equity vs an aggressive, deep-stacked button and just bloats the pot; shoving is unnecessary and folding surrenders too much value. Calling keeps the pot manageable, lets me realize equity (set-mine) and play postflop with position disadvantage but against a wide opening range. Plan: call to see the flop; if I hit a set extract value, otherwise proceed cautiously and fold to heavy turn/river aggression.

I called preflop as a set-mine with 4♥4♠. The flop A♥ Q♥ 3♦ misses me — no set and only a runner‑runner heart possibility. With ~28.7BB effective I want to keep the pot small and realize equity rather than bloating the pot out of position. Checking controls the size, preserves my ~8.4% chance to hit a set by the river, and lets me fold to heavy aggression. Plan: check now; call a small-to-medium continuation bet to continue set-mining, but fold to large bets or significant turn pressure unless I improve to a set. If the button checks back I’ll reevaluate on the turn and only bet/raise if I hit or have a clear, cheap line to take it down.

Preflop as button raiser with J5s suited connectors, raised for initiative and speculative value, called by Minni from BB (wide defending range likely including Ax, Qx, suited broadways, pairs, draws). Flop Ah Qh 3d is coordinated with flush draw, A/Q top pair potential, straight draws (KJT). My hand is complete air: no pair, no strong draw (spade backdoor, distant gutshot to 4-6 but unlikely). However, checked to me as aggressor in position vs single opponent with ~28BB. Standard continuation bet to represent Ax/Qx/sets, exploit her checking range (weak/missed hands fold, while value hands may call/shove). She folds often to aggression postflop (e.g., hands 16,27,29,33). Small sizing to ~1/2 pot denies equity, gets value from floats/draws, balanced with fold equity. Avoid checking as it gives free cards to her draws/OESD. Bet 325 (~1/2 pot) optimal balance.

I called preflop as a set‑mine with 4♥4♠ and checked the flop to control the pot. Elon’s 325 into the 650 pot is a small/medium c‑bet — large enough to make pure set‑mining marginal by odds alone, but small enough that calling is the right, low‑variance play OOP. Calling keeps the pot manageable, realizes my equity (I still have backdoor heart possibilities and an ~8.4% chance to hit a set by the river plus showdown equity against many missed hands in his range), and preserves the option to fold to heavy turn/river pressure if I don’t improve. Raising here would bloat the pot out of position and gain little fold equity; folding surrenders too much. Plan: call 325; if I hit a 4 on turn or river extract max value, if the turn brings heavy aggression and I’m unimproved I’ll fold.

Check. I flatted preflop as a set-mine and called Elon’s small flop c‑bet; the turn 8♥ does not improve my 4♥4♠ — I now have only two outs to hit a set (4♣, 4♦), ~4.3% on the river. Betting OOP here to represent a better hand (or a flush) has very little fold equity versus Elon's wide opening range and just bloats the pot with a very marginal holding. Elon apparently elected pot control on the turn, so checking keeps the pot manageable with ~25.5BB effective and preserves the option to fold to significant river aggression or call small river bets; if I hit a set I’ll extract value, otherwise I’ll concede to heavy action.

Turn board completes the nut flush draw (hearts) with 8h, adding significant scare cards and completing possible straights/draws, improving my bluffing spot as preflop raiser and flop c-bettor. My J5s is pure air (J-high, no pair/draw), but position and initiative represent Ax, Qx, sets, two-pair, or flushes strongly. Minni's check-call flop likely weaker Ax (no heart), Qx, mid-pairs, or missed draws; turn check suggests not improved to flush or monster. She's short-stacked (~25BB, 2550 behind) and folds frequently to aggression postflop (e.g., Hands 16,27,29,33,40,47). Pot 1300 offers great fold equity; bet sizing ~3/4 pot (1000) pressures her effective stack without overbetting, denies equity to her floats/draws, builds pot for river if called weakly. Checking concedes pot too often to her showdown value. Optimal semi-bluff continuation to exploit her tendencies and maintain balanced range.

Fold. I flatted preflop and the flop as a set‑mine with 4♥4♠ and checked the turn. Elon’s 1,000 into a 2,300 pot requires ~30.3% equity to justify a call (1000/(2300+1000)). I have only two clean outs to a set (2/46 ≈ 4.3%) plus nine hearts to make a flush (9/46 ≈ 19.6%) — ≈11 outs total or ~23.9% naive equity, which is below the break‑even threshold. Many of those heart outs are not “clean” (a heart river often gives a better flush to an opponent who holds a heart), and his line (button open, small flop c‑bet, now a sizable turn bet) is consistent with strong value. Raising OOP with a small pair is just pot‑bloated and speculative. Preserve the 2,550 stack for better spots — fold.

In a 3-handed game, K7o on the button is a standard opening hand. Minni is relatively short-stacked with 2500 chips, so I should be prepared for a potential shove, but K7o is generally ahead of a wide button-opening range. I will raise to 300 to take control of the pot and potentially take down the blinds.

I’m in the small blind with 6♣2♣ facing a button open to 300 (BB yet to act). This is one of the weakest starting hands — low, poorly connected and only a small suited chance. Calling 250 (~10% of my 2500 stack) to play a multiway pot out of position against a wide but still dominating opening range is negative EV. Raising (to 500 or shoving) would be a pure bluff with poor blockers and little fold equity against the big stacks behind me. With 25bb effective I should conserve chips and wait for a better spot. Fold.

K7o is a marginal hand at best from the BB facing a button raise to 3x. Offsuit, dominated by better kings and pairs in opponent's wide button opening range (which likely includes all Kx, Ax, broadways, suited connectors, pairs). Equity against such a range is around 25-30% at best, but post-flop OOP playability is poor with reverse implied odds if we hit top pair weak kicker. Pot odds are 450:200 (~2.25:1, ~31% break-even), but we don't have the equity or implied odds to justify calling. No reason to 3-bet bluff here as stacks are deep and opponent may defend wide. Folding preserves stack for better spots.

I’m on the button with Kc8s and 25BB facing only the blinds (pot 150, 100 to call). K8s is marginal but playable in position — suited king, good blockers and postflop playability. I want to take the initiative and apply fold equity, so I’ll open-raise rather than limp: a standard ~3xBB (raise to 300) narrows the field, discourages loose blind defense, and keeps the pot manageable given my 25BB stack. If I face a large isolation 3-bet I’ll fold (preserve stack); I can call a small 3-bet to see a flop because the hand plays well in position. Plan postflop: continuation-bet favorable textures, be cautious when facing heavy action on high-card boards where I’m likely dominated.

Preflop from SB facing a button raise to 300 (3x). Holding Q9o, which is offsuit and marginal at best. Button range is wide, but playing OOP multiway or heads-up postflop is tough with this hand. Equity likely around 30-35% against a loose button range, but poor playability OOP, dominated often by better Qx or suited connectors. Pot odds are 450:250 (~1.8:1), but implied odds limited with short-stacked Minni (2200). No strong history to exploit yet this hand. Standard fold to avoid difficult spots. Deep stack preserves chips for better opportunities over 150 hands.

I am in the Big Blind with King-Six suited. The button (Minni) has a short stack of 2,200 (22 big blinds) and has raised to 300. I need to call 200 more into a pot of 450, giving me 3.25:1 odds. King-Six suited is more than strong enough to defend against a button open at these odds. While Minni is short-stacked, calling allows me to see a flop and potentially outplay her post-flop or fold if I miss completely, rather than shoving and turning my hand into a pure equity flip against her calling range.

I have King-high on a Queen-high board. As the defender in the Big Blind, the standard play is to check to the pre-flop aggressor. Checking allows me to see how Minni acts on this board. Given her short stack (22 BBs), I need to be cautious about committing more chips without a piece of the board or a strong draw.

I continuation-bet half-pot (325). As the preflop raiser in position I want to apply pressure — the Qc-3c-9h flop is relatively dry and will miss a large portion of the big blind’s defending range (small pairs, broadway misses, weak aces), so a half-pot c-bet should fold enough hands to be profitable while keeping the pot manageable. I also hold Kc which blocks some club/top-card combos and gives a tiny backdoor flush/overcard equity. SPR (~3.4) means I don’t want to build a huge pot with just king-high, so I’ll size to 325. If Flash check‑raises big I’ll fold (likely Qx/pairs/strong club hands); if he calls I’ll re-evaluate the turn and exercise pot control unless I pick up real equity (a K or club).

I have King-high with a backdoor flush draw on a board of Qc-3c-9h. Minni, who is playing a relatively short stack of about 18 big blinds remaining, has made a continuation bet of 325 into a 650 pot. While I have two overcards to the 3 and the 9, I am out of position and have no direct equity or strong draws. Calling a 1/2 pot bet with King-high here against a short-stacked opponent who is likely to commit on the turn is not a profitable long-term play. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Preflop on the button in a 3-handed game with AKo, one of the strongest hands. Standard to raise for value and position. Raise to 300, a common sizing in this game (3x BB), to build the pot while keeping it manageable. AKo dominates most calling ranges of blinds, especially short-stacked Minni (2750). Deep effective stacks with Flash, but still great hand to play aggressively.

I am in the Small Blind with Jd6c. Elon, on the Button, has raised to 300. J6o is a very weak hand to play out of position against a button raise, especially since Minni in the Big Blind is short-stacked and might look to shove or defend wide. Folding is the most prudent and standard play here to avoid getting involved in a potentially losing situation with a marginal hand.

I’m in the big blind with JsTs against Elon’s button open to 300 in a 3-handed game. I have ~27.5BB (2750 chips) — JTs is a strong, playable hand with good blockers and postflop potential. Elon opens wide from the button frequently, so I want to apply pressure and pick up the pot or go heads-up with initiative. A small min‑reraise (to 500) is too weak; an over‑tight line (shove) is unnecessarily high variance. I’ll 3-bet to 900 (9BB): large enough to get fold equity and fold out many marginal hands, but small enough to leave ~1850 (≈18.5BB) behind to play postflop if called. Plan if called: play the flop aggressively when favorable and pot-control or fold to heavy action on highly unfavorable boards. If Elon 4-bets big, I’ll often fold this holding to avoid getting into dominated all-in spots.

Facing a 3-bet from short-stacked Minni (1950 total stack, ~10BB behind after her 900 raise). AKo is one of the best hands preflop, with excellent equity against her likely strong BB 3-bet range (pairs, AK/AQ, some bluffs). Pot odds favorable at ~2:1 direct + implied, but raising maximizes EV by denying equity, applying fold equity (she's committed but can fold bluffs), and isolating for value. Her sizing is large, suggesting polarization. 4-bet to her stack size (~1950 total) puts her all-in, standard vs short 3-bettor. Deep stack means no risk, position postflop if called. Folding/calling too weak; bet pot builds but raise better.

I 3‑bet to 900 with JTs to apply pressure and play postflop, but Elon’s 4‑bet to 1950 is a large, polarizing move that leaves me needing to call 1050 into a 2900 pot. That requires ~26.6% equity to break even. JTs is a playable hand, but OOP and facing a 4‑bet this size it’s often dominated by the value-heavy portion of a 4‑bet range (AA/KK/QQ/JJ/AK/strong AQ), where my equity is closer to the low‑20% range — below the pot‑odds threshold. While Elon can 4‑bet light sometimes, the size and dynamics here make a call effectively an all‑in with marginal equity and poor postflop position. Following my pre‑hand plan to avoid dominated all‑in spots and preserve my stack for better spots, the +EV play is to fold.

I am on the button with 2d 5c, which is one of the weakest starting hands in poker. Minni is short-stacked in the small blind and might be looking for a spot to shove, and Elon has a massive stack in the big blind. There is no strategic value in playing this hand, even in a short-handed game. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

I’m on the button (small blind) with 8♥6♣ — a marginal hand but in heads-up I have postflop position and should open a wide range. A standard 2xBB raise (to 200 total) takes the initiative, builds fold equity, and lets me play positionally on most flops. My stack is ~19BB so this small open costs little relative to potential reward; plan is to fold to a large 3-bet but continue postflop if Elon merely calls. Calling is too passive and folding surrenders the pot for no reason, so I’ll raise to 200.

AQo is a premium hand that dominates much of the small blind's wide min-raise range, which includes weaker aces, broadway cards, suited connectors, and speculative hands given Minni's position and short stack of ~17.5BB. Her raise to 200 is small, offering good implied odds if I call, but a standard 3-bet to 600 applies pressure, isolates if she calls, builds a pot for value against worse, and folds out junk that could flop draws. Post-flop OOP heads-up vs SB is manageable with this strong hand, and my deep stack (241x BB) vs her short one favors realizing equity. Folding is too tight; flat-calling risks multiway if Button wakes up (though folded). Raising maximizes EV over 150 hands.

I opened to 200 with 8♥6♣ in heads-up position, Elon 3-bet to 600. Pot is ~800 and calling 400 is asking me to commit ~25% of my ~17.5 BB stack for a spot where my hand is poorly positioned and often dominated. 8-6 offsuit has very little equity vs a typical 3-bet range (strong broadways, pairs, some bluffs) and plays poorly postflop. A 4-bet to 1000 would be a large, thin bluff and unnecessarily commits a big chunk of my stack. Per my preflop plan (open wide but fold to a large 3-bet), this is a fold — preserve chips and wait for a better, playable spot or shove/facing weaker action later.

I'm on the button with J♦4♣ and a stack of 1,750 (~17.5 BB). This is a marginal hand, but in a 3-handed pot the button should open a wide range to seize initiative and leverage position. A standard 3x BB open to 300 maximizes fold equity (often taking the pot preflop) while keeping my risk reasonable given my stack — I don't want to shove with a weak offsuit J. If I face a large 3-bet I will fold (J4o plays poorly bloated off-pot stacks), but if called I can use position to realize equity and apply postflop pressure.

Qs6d is a marginal suited hand OOP in SB facing a button raise to 300 (3BB). Minni's stack is short at 1450 (~14BB), making their raise committed, but my hand has poor playability multiway with BB behind and terrible position. Pot odds are 450:250 (~1.8:1), but equity vs wide button range (~35-40%) is insufficient OOP where I struggle postflop. Folding preserves deep stack (24375) for better spots in this long 150-hand game. No strong history indicating Minni is folding to 3-bets often enough to justify.

I hold Kh Qh in the Big Blind. Minni, who is on the Button, has a very short stack of 1450 chips (14.5 big blinds) and has raised to 300. This hand is well ahead of a typical button opening range. Given the shallow effective stacks, I should aim to get all the money in pre-flop. Raising to 1450 effectively puts Minni all-in and maximizes the pressure on her wide range while realizing the equity of my strong starting hand.

I opened wide on the button with J♦4♣ (300). Flash’s large 3‑bet to 1,450 effectively puts me all‑in or out — I must call 1,150 more into a 1,800 pot. That requires ~39% equity (1,150 / (1,800+1,150) ≈ 39%), which J4o simply doesn’t have versus a typical 3‑bet (value‑heavy) range and is frequently dominated. I can’t make a legal min‑raise to 1,750 (stack too small), and shoving as a bluff with no meaningful blockers is a poor risk/reward spot. With only ~14.5 BB behind it’s better to preserve the stack and wait for a better +EV opportunity, so I fold.

J8 suited is a strong speculative hand from the button, with great position and playability postflop. Pot is small at 150 with blinds posted (to call 100), Minni is short-stacked at 1350 (~13BB), Flash deeper but in SB. Standard button steal raise to isolate or take it down preflop. Raising to 300 (3x BB) is sized well to build pot or fold out weak hands. My deep stack allows flexibility.

Pocket fives are a solid hand in three-handed play, especially against a button open which typically represents a wide range. By raising from the small blind, I am taking the initiative and putting pressure on both Elon (the initial raiser) and Minni (the short stack in the big blind). If Elon folds, I am well-positioned against Minni's short-stack shoving range, and if both fold, I take down the pot immediately.

Elon opened to 300 and Flash 3-bet to 1000. Pot is 1,400 and I must call 900 (would commit 1,000 total). I hold T♠6♦ — a weak, often-dominated hand versus a SB 3-bet and BTN open. To justify a call I need roughly 39% equity (900 to win ~2,300), and T6o has far less against a reasonable 3-bet range. I could technically shove all-in for 1,350 (short of the min-raise number) but that’s a marginal shove that will be called by many hands that dominate me. With only ~13.5 BB and poor equity, the correct play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

J8d is a strong suited connector with excellent playability heads-up from the button with position on SB. Flash's SB 3-bet to 1000 (3.3x over my 300) after button open is polarized: value (QQ+, AK) or bluffs/speculative hands (Axs, suited connectors, weaker broadways). My hand has good equity against this range (~40-45% vs typical SB 3-bet range), hits straights, flush draws, pairs with backdoors. Pot odds are great: 700 to call into 1400 pot (2:1 direct, better implied). Deep stacks (me 24k, Flash 13k) favor realizing equity postflop in position. Folding is too tight for button vs SB; 4-betting speculative hand risks commitment OOP or postflop discomfort if called/jammed. Plan: call, see flop, c-bet weak boards or float strong, exploit position.

I am the pre-flop aggressor after 3-betting from the Small Blind. The flop of 3h-4h-Jc is relatively safe, though the Jack is an overcard to my pocket fives. There is a flush draw present. I will make a standard continuation bet of around 40% of the pot to maintain initiative, protect my marginal made hand against overcards (like KQ or AT), and potentially end the hand here. If Elon has a Jack or a higher pocket pair, I can re-evaluate on the turn.

Preflop: J8d suited is playable from button, called SB 3-bet for position and equity. Flop hits top pair (J) with weak 8 kicker, backdoor diamond draw. Board has heart FD and wheel SD possible. Flash c-bets 800 (~1/3 pot) into 2900, consistent with value (overpairs TT+, AQ+, sets) or FD/SD. My top pair has good showdown value and blocks J, equity ~45-55% vs his range. Position allows controlling pot OOP opponent. Pot odds excellent (800 into 3700, 4.6:1 implied better). Raising risks bloating pot OOP or isolating worse; fold too tight as hand realizes equity well. Call keeps pot manageable (3700), see turn cheap, exploit position to barrel turns/brick rivers or check behind value. Deep stacks (me 23k, opp 12k) favor playing postflop. Minni out, heads-up continues.

The turn is a Queen of Spades, which is another overcard to my pocket fives. Elon called my continuation bet on the flop, which suggests he has at least a piece of the board (like a Jack, a heart draw, or even a straight draw with 56). With two overcards (J, Q) now on the board and the fact that Elon called the flop, my pocket fives are likely behind or at best in a very marginal spot. I will check to see how he reacts. If he bets, I will likely have to fold as his range contains many Jacks, Queens, and better pairs, while I have very little showdown value and limited improvement potential.

Turn board Qs completes some broadway straight draws (e.g., KTs, T9s) and adds a spade, but no flush complete. Board remains drawy with lingering heart FD and wheel SD. My Jd8d is top pair top kicker? No, top pair weak 8 kicker, blocks Js, backdoor diamond. Flash's SB 3-bet pre, flop c-bet, turn check suggests: missed flop draw, medium strength (Ax, 77-TT, weak Q), or trapping nuts (sets, 34, QT). His check weakens range (less likely strong value continues aggression OOP), equity ~55-65% vs his polarized range (beats bluffs/draws, behind 2-pair+, overpairs, straights). Pot 3700, position, deep stacks (me 22k, him 12k). Check pot control risks free river cards improving him. Thin value bet extracts from worse (Qh, Ah, lower pairs, stubborn draws) while protecting. Size 1/2 pot ~1850, but round to 2000 for commitment. If raised/shoved, fold to monsters given weak kicker. Consistent with calling flop for realization/value now. Opponents aggressive per history (Flash pots big pots), exploit check as weakness.

I hold pocket fives on a board with two overcards (Jack and Queen). I was the pre-flop aggressor and continuation bet the flop, but Elon called. The Queen on the turn is a bad card for my range and likely improved Elon's holdings or gave him a strong reason to bet if he was floating with a hand like Qx or Jx. My fives have very little showdown value now and almost no equity to improve on the river against his perceived range. Calling a large bet of 2000 into a 3700 pot with such a weak hand is not profitable. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I am on the button with 9s3c, which is a very weak hand. In a 3-handed game, this hand is significantly below the opening range. Additionally, Minni in the Small Blind is very short-stacked (13 Big Blinds), making it likely she will shove or commit if I raise. There is no reason to play this hand.

Heads-up on the button (small blind) with A♣T♥ — a strong, playable hand heads-up that plays very well in position. The pot is 150 and it only costs 50 to call, but raising to the table minimum (to 200) is the right play: it takes the initiative, folds out weaker hands and dominated aces, builds a pot when I have a clear equity advantage, and gives me position postflop to realize that advantage. The raise size is standard and keeps the SPR manageable with my 1.3k stack. If Elon 3-bets big I can fold or re-evaluate; if he calls I can proceed with a c-bet or a controlled line depending on the flop texture. Raise to 200 to play the hand aggressively in position.

Minni, with a short stack of 1150 (~11.5BB), raised to 200 from the SB, which is a standard raise size but her range is likely wide given position and stack size (Ax, broadway, suited connectors, pairs, etc.). K7o is a marginal hand OOP, but it blocks KK/AA/KQ/KJ and has decent equity (~40-45%) against a wide SB opening range. Pot odds are excellent: 300 in pot to call 100 (effective pot odds 4:1 after). Post-flop, while OOP, my deep stack allows control, and her short stack limits her play. Folding is too tight given odds and equity; 3-betting risks bloating pot OOP with a hand that plays poorly multiway or vs shove; calling realizes equity well and keeps pot small.

I have A♣T♥ on 8♠ 8♣ T♣ — top pair with the best kicker and a backdoor club. Elon called my preflop raise, so his range is wide (many A‑highs, broadways, suited connectors and small pairs) and I’m beating a lot of that range. The flop is relatively dry (paired board, few real draws), so a value c‑bet is correct to extract from worse tens and A‑high hands, deny free cards, and keep the SPR manageable with my ~11.5bb stack. I’ll bet about half the pot (200). If he flats I can continue cautiously on the turn; if he check‑raises or shoves big I’ll fold most of my range since that line polarizes him to trips/sets/very strong tens.

Facing a 200 bet into a 600 pot on a paired 8s8cTc flop with K♥ 7♠, which has zero equity: no pair, no draws (no straight draw on 88T, no flush draw), pure bluff catcher at best but dominated by any king or ace in her SB c-betting range, overpairs, sets, or two-pair combos. Pot odds are ~3:1 (200 to win 800), requiring ~20% equity, but K7s realizes far less vs her value-heavy or semi-bluff range (88+, T8s+, AT+, KQ/KT, flush/straight draws like J9s/Q9s/97s). Preflop call was loose OOP with marginal hand expecting equity realization, but total brick on paired board warrants folding to avoid spewing deep stack chips. Her short stack (950) doesn't change this; jamming over a raise commits her but risks too much EV loss long-term vs aggressive SB. History shows Minni cbets wide but protected by board texture. Clear fold to preserve stack for better spots.

Short stack (1,550 ≈ 15.5bb) on the button with Q♥7♠. With ~15–16bb the push/fold strategy is optimal — limp/call to see a flop is too passive and a small raise (to 200) gives little fold equity and can get you 3-bet into a tough spot. Shoving maximizes fold equity against the blinds, uses the Queen blocker to reduce opponents' strong Qx/broadway combos, and simplifies decisions if called. I’ll shove my stack all-in to apply maximum pressure and pick up the blinds or go to a single all-in resolution.

Preflop in SB with JTo offsuit facing a massive button raise to 1550 (15.5BB) into a small pot. This is an extreme overraise suggesting a premium hand from Minni, who has shown aggression with strong holdings in prior hands. JTo plays poorly OOP multiway potentially (Flash in BB could call/raise), with poor implied odds and dominated often. Pot odds are ~2:1 but equity realization OOP is low vs tight range. Folding preserves stack for better spots; no need to speculate with marginal offsuit broadway.

I am in the Big Blind with Ac Jc. Minni has raised to 1550 from the button, which appears to be an all-in move given her stack size of 0 in the status. Ac Jc is a premium hand in a 3-handed game and is significantly ahead of a standard button shoving range, which often includes any Ace, many Kings, and various suited connectors or pairs. Calling 1450 more to win a pot of 1700 is a high-equity decision.

Heads-up on the button/SB with 5d8c offsuit, a very weak hand with poor connectivity, no high cards, and offsuit. While SB ranges are wide heads-up (~45-60% hands), 58o falls below typical raising range due to dominated potential and bad postflop playability. Calling 50 into 150 offers ~23% equity needed, but realization low postflop. Folding saves chips for better spots; dead money from blind is minimal relative to deep stacks (26k effective). Opponent Flash defends BB variably but exploits weak limps. Optimal: fold.

I have Ace-Four of Hearts on the button in a heads-up situation against Elon. This is a strong suited Ace that is well within my opening range. I want to build a pot and take the initiative, or simply win the blinds if Elon folds. Raising to 300 is a standard opening size.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button raise to 300 (200 to call into 400 pot). My hand is 6s7d, offsuit connectors with straight potential and decent playability heads-up. Button ranges are wide, especially from late position based on Flash's history of frequent raises and aggression (e.g., Hands 51,55 where he raised). Pot odds are excellent (3:1 direct, implied odds deep with my 25k stack vs his 13k). Folding is too tight in BB defense; 3-betting offsuit connectors risks domination without fold equity. Calling keeps pot small, realizes equity multi-street with position disadvantage but strong postflop potential on suited/straight draws. Plan to play aggressively if flop hits.

Flop Qs Jc Js completely misses my 67o - no pair, no straight draw (need 89T for potential straight), weak spade backdoor flush draw at best. Villain's button raising range is wide but hits this paired, drawy board often (overpairs, KQ, AJ, sets, two-pair combos). Out of position with 25k effective stacks (him 13k), but zero showdown value and minimal equity (~10-20% vs typical cbet range). Bluffing here (bet or check-raise) is low EV: board texture reduces fold equity (many calls with draws/medium strength), and history shows Flash calls aggressively (e.g., Hand 21, 43). Best to check-fold, preserve stack for better spots. Preflop call was speculative; this is the disciplined fold.

On a flop of Qs-Jc-Js, the board is paired and has a high card. While I have Ace-high, which has some showdown value, betting into this board texture is risky as Elon could easily have a Jack or a Queen in his defending range. Checking back allows me to control the size of the pot and see a free turn card, where I might improve or be able to evaluate Elon's action on a later street.

Turn card 7h pairs my 7d for middle pair with a mediocre kicker on a paired Js board (Qs Jc Js 7h). Preflop, called button raise speculatively with 67o; flop QsJcJs missed completely (no pair/draw), checked planning fold to bet, but Flash checked behind, indicating possible weakness (missed overcards, weak pairs, air) rather than trapping monsters given his aggressive history. Now OOP with ~13k effective stacks, pot 600. Middle pair has showdown value vs his wide checking range (beats bluffs/small pairs/A-high), decent equity (~40-50% vs polarized range), blocks some straight draws (89). River could complete spades/flush draws or T9 straight, so thin value bet protects and extracts from worse (77+, worse pairs). Small bet size ~2/3 pot builds pot without overcommitting; induces folds from air or calls from dominated hands. Checking risks giving free card to draws or check-raise, and pot control suboptimal with value. Betting exploits his flop check weakness per history (e.g., folds to aggression post-check like Hand 52). Plan: bet 400, fold to raise.

On a paired board of Qs-Jc-Js-7h, my Ace-high has some showdown value, but calling a 2/3 pot bet on the turn after checking back the flop is risky. Elon's lead-out after the flop check-check often represents a Jack, a Queen, or a middle pair that he's now betting for protection or value. While I could be ahead of pure bluffs, I have no draw to improve other than hitting an Ace, and even then, I might be behind a Jack. Given the board texture and the bet size, I'll fold and look for a more favorable spot.

Heads-up pre-flop on the button/SB position. My hand 4s3d is offsuit and low, but heads-up ranges are wide. Completing the SB for 50 into a pot of 150 offers excellent implied odds (calling 50 to win 200, better than 4:1). I have position post-flop and deep stacks relative to pot. Folding is too tight heads-up; raising is unnecessary with a speculative hand that plays well multiway or for set-mining/gutshots. Limping keeps pot small and realizes equity.

I have Ace-King of clubs, a premier starting hand. My opponent, Elon, limped from the small blind/button. I should raise to build the pot and take the initiative with my strong holding. A standard raise to 400 (4x the big blind) is appropriate here to punish the limp and define the hand range.

Facing an isolation raise from Flash in the BB after my SB limp. 43o is a speculative hand at best, but OOP post-flop with no initiative and poor equity against a wide but strong iso-range (pairs 66+, Ax, suited broadways/connectors, etc.). Pot odds are 500:300 (~1.67:1, needing ~37% equity), but 43o realizes far less than that OOP deep-stacked. Folding preserves stack and avoids marginal post-flop decisions where I'm crushed often. Tighten up vs aggression.
In a heads-up situation from the button, J7o is a standard opening hand. I will raise to 300 to take control of the pot and utilize my positional advantage post-flop.

T8o is a marginal offsuit hand, not connected well, and I'm out of position against a button raise from an aggressive player like Flash. Pot odds are 3:1 (200 to call into 400 pot becoming 600), but implied odds are poor OOP with this hand. Flash's range is wide but includes many strong hands that dominate mine. Standard play is to fold and preserve stack for better opportunities.

Heads-up on the button/SB with Q9o, a strong playable hand with good equity against BB calling range (around 60%+ equity). Standard strategy is to raise frequently from this position to steal blinds or build pot with initiative. Opponent Flash has shown aggression in past hands but folds to raises often when out of position. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) is a standard size, balancing value and bluff. Deep stacks allow for post-flop playability. Folding or limping is suboptimal.

I am in the Big Blind with 7d 5h. Elon has raised to 300 from the Button/Small Blind. While 75o is a speculative hand, Elon has been quite aggressive in late position. However, out of position, 75o is too weak to defend against a 3x raise consistently, especially with a stack size where I want to preserve chips for better spots. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

In this heads-up situation as the small blind, I am holding Q5o. While not a premium hand, it has reasonable high-card value. Elon in the big blind has a significant chip lead. To maintain pressure and not become too predictable by folding, I will put in a standard raise to 300 to try and take the pot pre-flop or define his hand.

Qs6h is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability and no strong potential. Facing a button small blind raise from Flash, who frequently raises wide from late position to steal blinds based on hand history (e.g., Hands 15, 17, 27, 33, 51, 62), defending out of position with this hand is -EV. Pot odds are insufficient (calling 200 into 400 pot for ~6bb), and post-flop I'll be OOP with a hand that rarely improves strongly. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots.

Heads-up preflop on the button/SB with 3c4h, a trash hand with very low equity and poor playability out of position postflop. Deep stacks (260 BB effective), no need to get involved with the worst part of the range. Standard play is to fold the bottom of the range like 34o to preserve stack and wait for better opportunities. Completing the SB gives poor implied odds with this uncoordinated holding.
I am on the button with Q8o. This is a standard opening hand in a heads-up or short-handed situation. I will raise to 300 to take control of the pot and apply pressure on the big blind.

Q5o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity against an aggressive Button/SB raise range. Out of position in the BB facing a standard raise, folding is the disciplined play to avoid difficult postflop spots on a small pot. Preserves stack for better opportunities.

Heads-up on the button (SB) vs BB. My hand Ks6s is suited king-high, strong enough to open wide in position. Deep stacks, but effective ~14k. Raising builds pot, steals blinds often, and takes initiative. Standard raise size 3x BB (300 total). Folding too tight, limping too passive. Opponent history shows they defend but fold to aggression often.
